2605.17359.title: Learning Transferable Topology Priors for Multi-Agent LLM Collaboration Across Domains
2605.17359.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM)-based multi-agent systems have shown strong potential for complex reasoning by coordinating specialized agents through structured communication. However, existing topology-evolution methods typically construct or optimize a collaboration topology for each query from scratch, leading to substantial online search overhead, high inference-time token consumption, and limited scalability in multi-domain settings. We propose TopoPrior, a framework for learning transferable topology priors for multi-agent LLM collaboration across domains. Rather than repeatedly searching for effective collaboration structures online, TopoPrior learns reusable topology priors from reference collaboration graphs collected offline from multiple domains and uses them to generate query-conditioned initial collaboration graphs for downstream refinement. By shifting part of topology search from per-query online optimization to offline prior learning, TopoPrior amortizes search cost while remaining compatible with existing topology-evolution backbones. Technically, TopoPrior contains two key components. First, a transferable topology prior learning module employs a conditional variational graph framework to capture reusable structural regularities across domains in a latent space. Second, a query-conditioned latent adaptation module introduces adversarial alignment to reduce unnecessary domain discrepancy while preserving query-relevant structural variation. Experiments on multi-domain reasoning benchmarks show that TopoPrior consistently improves several heterogeneous topology-evolution backbones while reducing online inference-time token usage, with only modest additional trainable parameters. These results suggest that transferable topology initialization is an effective and lightweight mechanism for improving the efficiency of multi-agent LLM collaboration across domains.
2605.17359.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLM) 基礎的多代理系統已顯示出通過結構化通信協調專門代理進行複雜推理的強大潛力。<lb/>然而，現有的拓撲演化方法通常為每個查詢從頭開始構建或優化協作拓撲，這導致了大量的在線搜索開銷、高推理時間的標記消耗以及在多領域設置中的有限擴展性。<lb/>我們提出了 TopoPrior，一個用於學習可轉移拓撲先驗的框架，旨在促進跨領域的多代理 LLM 協作。<lb/>TopoPrior 不會重複在線搜索有效的協作結構，而是從多個領域離線收集的參考協作圖中學習可重用的拓撲先驗，並利用這些先驗生成查詢條件的初始協作圖以進行下游精煉。<lb/>通過將部分拓撲搜索從每個查詢的在線優化轉移到離線先驗學習，TopoPrior 在保持與現有拓撲演化骨幹兼容的同時，攤銷了搜索成本。<lb/>在技術上，TopoPrior 包含兩個關鍵組件。<lb/>首先，可轉移拓撲先驗學習模塊採用條件變分圖框架來捕捉跨領域的可重用結構規律，並在潛在空間中進行建模。<lb/>其次，查詢條件的潛在適應模塊引入對抗性對齊，以減少不必要的領域差異，同時保留與查詢相關的結構變化。<lb/>在多領域推理基準上的實驗顯示，TopoPrior 一直在提高幾個異質拓撲演化骨幹的性能，同時減少在線推理時間的標記使用，僅增加了適度的可訓練參數。<lb/>這些結果表明，可轉移的拓撲初始化是一種有效且輕量的機制，用於提高跨領域的多代理 LLM 協作效率。
2605.17359.author: Taolin Zhang et.al.
2605.17359.authors: Taolin Zhang, Zijie Zhou, Jiuheng Wan, Tingyuan Hu, Chengyu Wang, Xiaofeng He, Richang Hong
